scope.files_unchanged_audit_disposition: Prior style-inspector audit verdict (0 critical, 2 advisories on the 3 original files) carried forward unchanged; those files were not re-read this round per OUT_OF_SCOPE contract from the orchestrator.
scope.ac_basis: close.md AC-2.6 cleanliness-of-THIS-diff scoping; line-level default-safe rule

standards_evaluated.name: no-inline-code
standards_evaluated.rationale: All 3 changed files are .py; no command/agent markdown affected.

standards_evaluated.applicability: shell .sh and command .md that invoke python
standards_evaluated.rationale: Changed files are Python sources, not invocation sites. Grepped each file for 'python3 ' patterns — 0 hits.

standards_evaluated.name: meaningful-naming
standards_evaluated.rationale: All identifiers are verb-phrase or noun-phrase descriptive. None matches forbidden patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, *-v2, optimize-*, improved-*, better-*, new-*). _resolve_base_context's 'base' prefix is semantically meaningful — explicitly distinguishes from _resolve_context's strict-gate variant. _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step is long but precisely encodes its contract; per codex consultation this is NOT a meaningful-naming violation (OBSERVATION_ONLY there).

standards_evaluated.name: english-only
standards_evaluated.rationale: Grepped each of the 3 files for Unicode range U+4E00..U+9FFF; 0 hits.

standards_evaluated.name: merge-similar-scripts
standards_evaluated.rationale: No new script files created (per dev-report.files_created=[]). Function additions inside existing files do not implicate this standard.

standards_evaluated.name: parameterized-scripts
standards_evaluated.verdict: no critical violations; 1 advisory (K=3 magic number)

standards_evaluated.name: dangling-references
standards_evaluated.rationale: No .md files in this diff. Python imports inside .py files are internal (lib.todo_canonical, lib.contract_runtime) and pre-existing in the baseline; no new imports added in this cycle that could be dangling.

standards_evaluated.name: no-merge-conflict-markers
standards_evaluated.rationale: Ran ~/.claude/scripts/detect-merge-conflicts.sh and intersected with the 3 changed files — 0 hits. (The detector reports other repo-wide pre-existing markers in INDEX.md / hooks/README.md / etc., but none in the 3 cycle-diff files; those are not introduced_in_diff and are out of changed-files scope per AC-2.6.)

advisory_findings.finding: Magic number K=3 hardcoded inline: `end = min(len(canonical), start + 3)  # K=3 bounded window`. The window size is a BA-ticket-mandated constant (per dev-report.tasks_completed[1].changes — 'K=3 bounded window'), but inline literal-3 with only an end-of-line comment as documentation is weaker than a named module-level constant. Documented as advisory rather than critical because (a) the constant is design-mandated by the BA spec, (b) the inline value has a docstring reference in _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step at line 171 ('K=3 bounded window'), and (c) any tuning would require BA-ticket re-spec, so parameterization for environment-runtime override is not the goal.
advisory_findings.recommendation: Introduce module-level constant near the top of hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py: `SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW = 3  # BA cycle 20260519-211515 Item H` and replace `start + 3` with `start + SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW`. Naming per codex consultation: avoid opaque K_WINDOW; SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW or TYPED_LOOKUP_LOOKAHEAD is preferred.
advisory_findings.disposition_rationale: Inspector reports; does not auto-fix. Advisory only — does not block close cleanliness check.

advisory_findings.finding: New AC-D1 regression tests embed the literal grant-file path `/tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{test_sid}.json` directly in test bodies (mirroring pre-existing baseline tests at lines 129, 139, 192, etc.). Per codex consultation, this is test-fixture coupling that matches the production grant-file contract literal in hooks/lib/allowlist.py:120 (`f'/tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{sid}.json'`). Not a critical violation because the production module uses the same literal as the canonical contract; advisory because both sides could be centralized into a single helper (e.g., `lib.allowlist.grant_path_for(sid)`).
advisory_findings.recommendation: Optionally extract a shared helper `lib.allowlist.grant_path_for(sid: str) -> Path` and use it from both production sites AND test fixtures, so the literal `/tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-*` lives in exactly one place. Not required for close-cleanliness.
advisory_findings.disposition_rationale: Inspector reports; advisory. Mirroring the pre-existing test pattern is acceptable — refactoring is a separate follow-up cycle concern.

observations.standard: documentation-accuracy (not one of the 11 critical standards)
observations.finding: TestReadGrant class docstring still says 'Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_or_substr semantics).' but this cycle changed read_grant's semantics to exact_only (allowlist.py:149). The docstring is at line 113 which is OUTSIDE the diff's changed-line range (per AC-2.6 line-level rule, this is `introduced_in_diff: false`). However, the new tests at lines 155-184 within the same class enforce exact_only semantics, making the class-level docstring inaccurate.
observations.recommendation: (Soft) Update class docstring at line 113 to 'Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_only literal semantics).' Logged for transparency; does NOT block close cleanliness per AC-2.6.
observations.disposition_rationale: Not a violation of any of the 11 enumerated standards. AC-2.6 line-level rule: line 113 is outside diff-overlap → introduced_in_diff is false → cannot be flagged as cycle-introduced. Codex flagged this as 'documentation accuracy miss' but explicitly noted it is not one of the 11 critical standards.

observations.standard: exception-handling (functional risk, NOT a style standard)
observations.finding: Codex raised that broad `except Exception: return None` in _try_load_contract is mostly pre-existing (not introduced by this cycle), but the cycle's new contract-presence-first routing now BRANCHES on the contract load outcome — meaning a load error (vs a clean absent contract) falls through to the legacy path with no diagnostic. This is a FUNCTIONAL risk note, not a style/cleanliness violation. The pre-diff baseline already had this same exception-swallowing behavior — the routing change merely makes the failure mode more visible.
observations.recommendation: (Out of scope for inspector — file as functional follow-up.) Consider distinguishing 'absent contract' (None from clean miss) vs 'load error' (Exception path) and no-writing on load error rather than falling through to legacy. Not part of any of the 11 style standards.
observations.disposition_rationale: Pre-existing code shape; AC-2.6 default-safe rule: line range is outside diff-overlap. Inspector scope is style standards 1-11; functional exception-handling concerns are QA territory. Logged for orchestrator awareness only.

observations.finding: Codex initially raised the long helper name as a possible naming concern, then explicitly downgraded to OBSERVATION_ONLY: 'long but precise; do not flag it as a blocker.' Recorded here for transparency.
observations.recommendation: (No change required.) The name precisely encodes the helper's contract (earliest + unflipped + matching + step). Standard 5 forbids generic enhancement words; long-but-precise verb_adj_phrase names are aligned with the rule's intent, not a violation.
observations.disposition_rationale: Explicitly downgraded by codex; not a Standard 5 violation.

verdict.summary: NO NEW CRITICAL VIOLATIONS introduced by cycle 20260519-211515 diff. Two advisory findings (K=3 magic number + test-fixture path coupling), both with introduced_in_diff=true and concrete PROPOSED_FIX. Three observations (documentation-accuracy class-docstring staleness, exception-handling functional risk, helper-name length) all logged for transparency but explicitly NOT critical violations. Per AC-2.6 default-safe rule, the report is suitable for close.md cleanliness gating: zero critical_violations means close-clean passes.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 6 challenges to the draft verdict. (1) K=3 advisory: report verdict was internally inconsistent ('NO NEW VIOLATIONS' but described an advisory) — corrected. (2) Constant name K_WINDOW is itself opaque — adopted SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW instead. (3) /tmp/... paths in new tests are newly-added hardcoded path literals; added as second advisory with introduced_in_diff=true. (4) _try_load_contract exception swallowing: pre-existing — logged as observation_only (out-of-scope functional risk). (5) Stale TestReadGrant class docstring: line 113 outside diff-overlap → introduced_in_diff=false per AC-2.6 → logged as observation_only. (6) Helper name length: explicitly downgraded by codex to OBSERVATION_ONLY.

findings.issue: Verdict was internally inconsistent: 'NO NEW VIOLATIONS' but text described an advisory K=3 finding.
findings.proposed_fix: Restate verdict as 'NO NEW CRITICAL VIOLATIONS; 1 advisory Standard-2/9 finding: replace inline K=3 with module constant SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW = 3.'
findings.rationale: Correct logical inconsistency. The verdict.summary and the advisory_findings array are now coherent: 0 critical, 2 advisories.

findings.proposed_fix: Use SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW = 3 or TYPED_LOOKUP_LOOKAHEAD = 3 in the recommendation, not K_WINDOW.
findings.rationale: Self-consistency: if we recommend a name in a Standard-5 sense we must follow Standard 5's spirit. Recommendation now reads SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW.

findings.issue: Draft claimed 'No new hardcoded paths introduced' but new tests at lines 158, 177 add /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{test_sid}.json literals.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword Standard-2 to: 'New tests add /tmp allowlist fixture paths matching the production grant-file contract; accepted as test-fixture coupling, but could be centralized via helper allowlist_path(sid).'
findings.rationale: Correctly factual. Added as second advisory finding with introduced_in_diff=true and matching diff_overlap=true.

findings.issue: _try_load_contract exception swallowing is risky given new contract-presence-first routing.
findings.proposed_fix: Add an out-of-scope functional-risk note: 'Broad contract load exception swallowing may fail open into legacy path; distinguish absent contract from load failure, and no-write on load failure.'
findings.rationale: Pre-existing code shape (lines 279-287); outside diff-overlap; functional concern not in the 11 style standards. Logged in observations[] for orchestrator transparency.

findings.issue: Stale TestReadGrant class docstring at line 113 still says 'exact_or_substr semantics' while the cycle changes read_grant to exact_only.
findings.proposed_fix: Change docstring to 'Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_only literal semantics).'
findings.rationale: Codex explicitly noted 'Not one of the 11 critical standards, but it is a documentation accuracy miss.' Per AC-2.6 line-level rule, line 113 is outside diff-overlap → introduced_in_diff=false → cannot be flagged as cycle-introduced critical. Logged in observations[] as soft transparency note.
Codex explicitly downgraded this: 'long but precise; do not flag it as a blocker.' OBSERVATION_ONLY per codex's own classification.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 6 codex findings addressed. 2 applied (K=3 advisory + constant name rewording + new test-path advisory). 3 logged as observations (exception-handling functional risk, stale docstring, helper-name length). 0 rejected. Verdict.summary now consistent with advisory_findings array; zero critical_violations; close_cleanliness_gate=pass.
